title: Remove sentinel active-transaction semantics from normal flow
summary: Implement the 0.6.0 runtime simplification that removes sentinel transaction-identity semantics such as `none` from normal canonical behavior. This ticket updates active-transaction representation, runtime checks, and continuation logic so the absence of active work is represented structurally rather than through fake transaction identifiers. The goal is to make begin, resume, verify, commit, and terminal handling depend on exact canonical state instead of sentinel text, reducing ambiguity and making interruption-safe continuation easier to reason about.
outputs: Runtime code that no longer uses sentinel transaction identifiers such as `none` in normal canonical behavior
A structural representation for the absence of active work in canonical transaction state
Updated begin, resume, verify, commit, and end checks that rely on exact canonical state rather than sentinel text
Updated rebuild and compatibility handling that preserves legacy historical data without using sentinel semantics for new runtime behavior
Regression coverage for no-active-transaction representation and sentinel-free continuation behavior
acceptance_criteria: Normal runtime behavior no longer uses sentinel transaction identifiers such as `none` to represent missing active work.
The absence of active work is represented structurally in canonical transaction state as `active_tx: null`, rather than by fake `tx_id` text or placeholder active-transaction objects.
Begin-required logic no longer depends on sentinel transaction identifiers in normal flow.
Resume logic no longer treats sentinel transaction identifiers as a normal source of truth for active-transaction decisions.
Verify, commit, and terminal continuation logic no longer depends on sentinel transaction identifiers in normal flow.
Runtime checks distinguish clearly between no active transaction, active non-terminal transaction, and terminal historical transaction state.
Historical legacy data containing sentinel-like values remains readable only under bounded compatibility handling and does not reintroduce sentinel semantics into new runtime behavior.

Regression tests cover at least: `active_tx: null` no-active representation, begin from no-active state, resume from active state, and compatibility behavior for legacy sentinel-bearing historical data if such data exists.
The implementation preserves canonical lifecycle ordering, resumability, replay integrity, and explicit terminal-state handling.
tasks: Identify runtime code paths that use sentinel transaction identifiers such as `none` to represent missing active work or to branch normal lifecycle behavior.
Define and implement the canonical structural no-active-transaction representation in transaction state as `active_tx: null`, matching the Phase 1 no-active invariant.
Update begin-required checks so they depend on structural absence of active work rather than sentinel transaction text.
Update resume checks so they use exact active transaction presence and status rather than sentinel-based normalization.
Update verify, commit, and terminal continuation checks so they do not rely on sentinel transaction identifiers in normal flow.

Update materialized-state serialization and deserialization as needed so no-active-transaction state remains explicit as `active_tx: null`, with no fake transaction identity and no placeholder active-transaction object.
Update rebuild logic so historical sentinel-bearing data is handled as bounded compatibility input rather than reintroduced as normal runtime semantics.
Update comparison and validation logic to distinguish clearly between missing active work, active non-terminal work, and terminal historical work.
Add or update regression tests covering sentinel-free no-active representation and continuation behavior.
Document any bounded compatibility rules required for historical data that may still contain sentinel transaction identifiers.
notes: This ticket is runtime implementation work, not only contract-definition work.
The most important outcome is that no-active-work state becomes structurally explicit and no longer encoded as fake transaction identity.
This ticket should simplify runtime decision-making by removing a class of special-case sentinel branching from normal flow.
Historical compatibility matters, but new runtime behavior should be sentinel-free.
The implementation should preserve exact active-transaction continuation and should not replace sentinel text with a different fake identity convention.

representation_rules.normal_no_active_state: Represent absence of active work structurally in canonical state as `active_tx: null` rather than by sentinel transaction identifiers, fake active objects, or placeholder transaction identity.
forbidden_normal_flow_examples: fake active transaction placeholders used as normal continuation inputs
empty-string or placeholder `active_tx` objects used to stand in for no active work
representation_rules.allowed_compatibility_scope: Historical data may still be readable under bounded compatibility handling, but new runtime behavior must not depend on sentinel semantics.

runtime_behavior_rules.begin_rule: New work may begin when there is structurally no active non-terminal transaction, represented canonically as `active_tx: null`.
runtime_behavior_rules.resume_rule: Resume must act on an exact active non-terminal transaction when present, not on sentinel placeholders.
runtime_behavior_rules.continuation_rule: Verify, commit, and end flows must use canonical transaction state and exact identity, not sentinel text.

state_distinctions.no_active_work: No active non-terminal transaction exists, represented canonically as `active_tx: null` with no placeholder active-transaction object and no stray active-transaction identity fields.
state_distinctions.active_work: One exact non-terminal active transaction exists and must be resumed before new work begins.
state_distinctions.terminal_history: Historical transaction data may exist, but no active resumable transaction remains.

compatibility_rules.historical_sentinel_data: Legacy sentinel-bearing historical data may be parsed or normalized under bounded compatibility behavior.
compatibility_rules.new_write_policy: No new materialized state or event-writing path may emit sentinel transaction identifiers as normal canonical state.

test_focus: begin from structurally empty active state
resume from exact active transaction state
bounded compatibility for legacy sentinel-bearing data

design_bias.preferred_anchor: Structural state plus exact active transaction identity.
avoid: sentinel transaction identifiers in normal flow
branching lifecycle behavior on fake transaction text
replacing one sentinel convention with another
mixing compatibility parsing with canonical new-write semantics

canonical_alignment.synchronization_intent: Use this ticket to remove sentinel-based active-transaction semantics from runtime behavior so the 0.6.0 protocol remains exact and structurally resumable.
canonical_alignment.current_canonical_constraint: Implementation must preserve the single-active-transaction model and must treat absence of active work structurally rather than through fake transaction identity.
